headline: Microsoft’s GitHub was positioned to win the AI coding race. Outages got in the way
subject: 🚨 AI Alert: 1 new launch — CNBC Technology
article_title: Microsoft’s GitHub was positioned to win the AI coding race. Outages got in the way

article_items.title: Microsoft’s GitHub was positioned to win the AI coding race. Outages got in the way
article_items.description: GitHub's user base has swelled under Microsoft's ownership, but the software repository has fallen behind newer rivals in the world of vibe coding.
article_items.excerpt: GitHub's user base has swelled under Microsoft's ownership, but the software repository has fallen behind newer rivals in the world of vibe coding.
article_items.summary: GitHub was there first, having announced Copilot in 2021 . The software repository market saw a surge in usage with the onset of AI-assisted coding, or vibe coding, as agentic AI allowed developers to ramp up their production. The practical impact: Microsoft had all the pieces to win in vibe coding, thanks to the near ubiquity of GitHub, which the company bought for $7.5 billion in 2018. “Right now people are tired of the instability, the product churn, the Copilot AI noise, the unclear leadership, and the feeling that the platform is no longer primarily designed for the community that made it valuable,” Armin Ronacher, creator of the... Watch next: “Obviously, GitHub also finds itself in the midst of the agentic coding revolution and that causes enormous pressure on the folks over there.
